cards.title: Therapeutic Diets — Overview & Selection

alerts.label: Core Principle
alerts.text: Diet is the most powerful tool in naturopathic medicine. The right therapeutic diet can reverse disease; the wrong one can worsen it. Selection must be based on diagnosis, constitution, digestive capacity, and patient preference.

rows: Remove gluten, dairy, eggs, soy, nuts sequentially
Autism, IBS, leaky gut, psychiatric conditions
Olive oil, fish, vegetables, legumes, whole grains
No animal products — supplement B12, D3, omega-3
Low GI, consistent carbs, high fibre
Low saturated fat, sodium <2g/day, soluble fibre

cards._legacyHtml:
Core Principle
Diet is the most powerful tool in naturopathic medicine. The right therapeutic diet can reverse disease; the wrong one can worsen it. Selection must be based on diagnosis, constitution, digestive capacity, and patient preference.
DietPrimary UseKey FeatureDuration
Anti-inflammatoryAutoimmune, arthritis, CVD, cancer preventionOmega-3 rich, colourful vegetables, spicesLifelong
Elimination dietFood allergy/intolerance, IBS, eczema, migraineRemove gluten, dairy, eggs, soy, nuts sequentially3–6 weeks + reintroduction
KetogenicEpilepsy, T2DM, obesity, PCOS, NAFLD<50g carbs/day, 70% fat, ketosis3–6 months supervised
GAPS dietAutism, IBS, leaky gut, psychiatric conditionsBone broth, ferments, removes grains/sugar6–24 months
Low-FODMAPIBS, SIBO, IBD, functional bloatingEliminates fermentable carbohydrates6–8 weeks restriction + reintroduction
MediterraneanCVD, T2DM, MetS, depression, longevityOlive oil, fish, vegetables, legumes, whole grainsLifelong
Plant-based/veganCVD, T2DM, obesity, cancer preventionNo animal products — supplement B12, D3, omega-3Lifelong
Renal dietCKD stages 3–5Low potassium, phosphorus, sodium, proteinOngoing with monitoring
Diabetic dietT1DM, T2DM, gestational diabetesLow GI, consistent carbs, high fibreLifelong
Cardiac dietPost-MI, heart failure, hyperlipidaemiaLow saturated fat, sodium <2g/day, soluble fibreLifelong

cards.title: Anti-Inflammatory Diet — Clinical Protocol

rows: Extra virgin olive oil, avocado, walnuts, flaxseed
Trans fats, refined vegetable oils (sunflower, corn)
Oily fish (salmon, sardines, mackerel) 3x/week, legumes, tofu
Whole grains, sweet potato, quinoa, oats
White flour, white rice, sugary foods, soft drinks
5+ servings colourful veg (broccoli, spinach, beetroot, turmeric)
Fruit juices, canned fruits in syrup
Turmeric 1tsp + black pepper daily, ginger, cinnamon, garlic
Green tea 2–3 cups, water 2.5–3L, herbal teas

pearls.label: 🟡 Pearl — Turmeric Protocol
pearls.text: Curcumin bioavailability is only 1% alone. Must combine with: (1) Black pepper (piperine) — increases absorption 2000%. (2) Fat — dissolves in lipids. (3) Heat — improves solubility. Standard clinical dose: 500–1000mg curcumin with piperine and fat-containing meal. Equivalent food dose: 1 tsp turmeric powder in golden milk with black pepper and coconut milk.

cards._legacyHtml:
CategoryInclude (daily)Reduce/Avoid
FatsExtra virgin olive oil, avocado, walnuts, flaxseedTrans fats, refined vegetable oils (sunflower, corn)
ProteinOily fish (salmon, sardines, mackerel) 3x/week, legumes, tofuProcessed meat, red meat >2x/week
CarbohydratesWhole grains, sweet potato, quinoa, oatsWhite flour, white rice, sugary foods, soft drinks
Vegetables5+ servings colourful veg (broccoli, spinach, beetroot, turmeric)Deep-fried vegetables
FruitsBerries (blueberry, pomegranate), citrus, papaya, amlaFruit juices, canned fruits in syrup
Spices ★Turmeric 1tsp + black pepper daily, ginger, cinnamon, garlicExcess salt (>5g/day)
DrinksGreen tea 2–3 cups, water 2.5–3L, herbal teasAlcohol, cola, excessive coffee
🟡 Pearl — Turmeric Protocol
Curcumin bioavailability is only 1% alone. Must combine with: (1) Black pepper (piperine) — increases absorption 2000%. (2) Fat — dissolves in lipids. (3) Heat — improves solubility. Standard clinical dose: 500–1000mg curcumin with piperine and fat-containing meal. Equivalent food dose: 1 tsp turmeric powder in golden milk with black pepper and coconut milk.

cards.title: Elimination Diet — Step-by-Step Protocol

pearls.label: 🟡 Pearl
pearls.text: Herxheimer reaction (detox reaction) is common in first 3–7 days: headache, fatigue, irritability, skin breakouts, loose stools. Warn patients beforehand. It typically resolves by day 5–7 and is a sign of detoxification, not intolerance. Ensure 3L water/day and adequate fibre during this phase.

steps.title: Phase 1 — Elimination (3 weeks)
steps.text: Remove ALL of: gluten (wheat, rye, barley), dairy, eggs, soy, corn, peanuts, tree nuts, shellfish, nightshades (tomato, pepper, potato, eggplant), alcohol, caffeine, processed food, refined sugar.

steps.title: Safe Foods During Elimination
steps.text: Rice, millet, sweet potato, all vegetables (except nightshades), most fruits, chicken, fish (non-shellfish), lamb, olive oil, coconut oil, rice milk, herbal teas.

steps.title: Phase 2 — Reintroduction (1 food every 3 days)
steps.text: Reintroduce one eliminated food every 3 days. Eat it 2–3x on day 1, then observe days 2–3. Document: digestive symptoms, skin changes, energy, mood, joint pain, nasal symptoms.

steps.title: Reintroduction Order
steps.text: Least allergenic first: gluten-free grains → legumes → nightshades → eggs → dairy → nuts → gluten. Leave gluten and dairy last as most commonly reactive.

steps.title: Phase 3 — Personalised Diet
steps.text: Create patient-specific diet excluding identified reactive foods. Review every 6 months — some intolerances are temporary (leaky gut, dysbiosis-related) and resolve with gut healing.

cards._legacyHtml:
1
Phase 1 — Elimination (3 weeks) Remove ALL of: gluten (wheat, rye, barley), dairy, eggs, soy, corn, peanuts, tree nuts, shellfish, nightshades (tomato, pepper, potato, eggplant), alcohol, caffeine, processed food, refined sugar.
2
Safe Foods During Elimination Rice, millet, sweet potato, all vegetables (except nightshades), most fruits, chicken, fish (non-shellfish), lamb, olive oil, coconut oil, rice milk, herbal teas.
3
Phase 2 — Reintroduction (1 food every 3 days) Reintroduce one eliminated food every 3 days. Eat it 2–3x on day 1, then observe days 2–3. Document: digestive symptoms, skin changes, energy, mood, joint pain, nasal symptoms.
4
Reintroduction Order Least allergenic first: gluten-free grains → legumes → nightshades → eggs → dairy → nuts → gluten. Leave gluten and dairy last as most commonly reactive.
5
Phase 3 — Personalised Diet Create patient-specific diet excluding identified reactive foods. Review every 6 months — some intolerances are temporary (leaky gut, dysbiosis-related) and resolve with gut healing.
🟡 Pearl
Herxheimer reaction (detox reaction) is common in first 3–7 days: headache, fatigue, irritability, skin breakouts, loose stools. Warn patients beforehand. It typically resolves by day 5–7 and is a sign of detoxification, not intolerance. Ensure 3L water/day and adequate fibre during this phase.

cards.title: Low-FODMAP Diet — Clinical Guide

alerts.label: FODMAP = Fermentable Oligosaccharides Disaccharides Monosaccharides And Polyols
alerts.text: These are short-chain carbohydrates that are poorly absorbed in small intestine → fermented by gut bacteria → gas, bloating, pain, altered bowel habit.

rows: Milk, yoghurt, soft cheese, ice cream
Hard cheese, lactose-free milk, almond milk
Banana, blueberry, strawberry, orange, maple syrup
Stone fruits (peach, plum), mushrooms, cauliflower, sugar-free gum

pearls.label: 🟡 Key Pearl
pearls.text: Garlic and onion are the most common FODMAP triggers — even small amounts cause symptoms. Safe alternatives: garlic-infused oil (FODMAP does not leach into oil), chives, green onion tops only (not the white bulb). Low-FODMAP reduces IBS symptoms in 70–75% of patients within 2 weeks. Always reintroduce after 6–8 weeks — lifelong restriction causes dysbiosis.

cards._legacyHtml:
FODMAP = Fermentable Oligosaccharides Disaccharides Monosaccharides And Polyols
These are short-chain carbohydrates that are poorly absorbed in small intestine → fermented by gut bacteria → gas, bloating, pain, altered bowel habit.
FODMAP TypeHigh (Avoid)Low (Safe)
Oligosaccharides (fructans/GOS)Wheat, garlic, onion, legumes, cashewsRice, oats (GF), quinoa, canned/rinsed chickpeas
Disaccharides (lactose)Milk, yoghurt, soft cheese, ice creamHard cheese, lactose-free milk, almond milk
Monosaccharides (fructose)Apple, mango, honey, HFCS, asparagusBanana, blueberry, strawberry, orange, maple syrup
Polyols (sorbitol/mannitol)Stone fruits (peach, plum), mushrooms, cauliflower, sugar-free gumCarrot, cucumber, zucchini, pineapple
🟡 Key Pearl
Garlic and onion are the most common FODMAP triggers — even small amounts cause symptoms. Safe alternatives: garlic-infused oil (FODMAP does not leach into oil), chives, green onion tops only (not the white bulb). Low-FODMAP reduces IBS symptoms in 70–75% of patients within 2 weeks. Always reintroduce after 6–8 weeks — lifelong restriction causes dysbiosis.

cards.title: Ketogenic & Plant-Based Diets — Clinical Protocols

alerts.label: Keto Macros: 70–75% fat, 20–25% protein, 5% carbs (<50g/day)
alerts.text: Induces nutritional ketosis (0.5–3.0 mmol/L) within 2–7 days. Fat = primary fuel.

rows: 6 months supervised. Reduces androgens, restores ovulation.
Significant hepatic fat loss within 2 weeks. Monitor LFTs.
Monitor ferritin annually. Bisglycinate 25mg + Vit C if ferritin <40

pearls.label: 🟡 Keto Flu (Days 1–7) Management
pearls.text: Electrolyte loss causes: headache, fatigue, muscle cramps. Prevention: sodium 3–5g/day (salt generously or bouillon broth), potassium from avocado/leafy greens, magnesium glycinate 400mg/day, 3L water/day. Contraindications: pyruvate carboxylase deficiency, porphyria, fat oxidation disorders, CKD (high protein load), pancreatitis, eating disorder history.

cards._legacyHtml:
KETOGENIC DIET
Keto Macros: 70–75% fat, 20–25% protein, 5% carbs (<50g/day)
Induces nutritional ketosis (0.5–3.0 mmol/L) within 2–7 days. Fat = primary fuel.
IndicationEvidenceProtocol Note
Epilepsy (drug-resistant) ★★★★★ Grade A — 50% seizure reduction in 50%4:1 fat:protein+carb. Strict supervision: neurologist + dietitian.
Type 2 Diabetes★★★★ Strong<30g carbs/day. Monitor medications — hypoglycaemia risk.
Obesity/Metabolic syndrome★★★★ Short-term strong. Long-term adherence challenging.Whole-foods approach. Not processed "keto snacks".
PCOS★★★ Good6 months supervised. Reduces androgens, restores ovulation.
NAFLD★★★ GoodSignificant hepatic fat loss within 2 weeks. Monitor LFTs.
🟡 Keto Flu (Days 1–7) Management
Electrolyte loss causes: headache, fatigue, muscle cramps. Prevention: sodium 3–5g/day (salt generously or bouillon broth), potassium from avocado/leafy greens, magnesium glycinate 400mg/day, 3L water/day. Contraindications: pyruvate carboxylase deficiency, porphyria, fat oxidation disorders, CKD (high protein load), pancreatitis, eating disorder history.
PLANT-BASED / VEGAN DIET — Essential Supplements
NutrientRiskSupplement Protocol
B12 ★HIGH — only in animal foodsMethylcobalamin 1000mcg/day sublingual — ESSENTIAL, never optional
Omega-3 DHA/EPAHIGH — ALA converts only 5–10%Algae-derived DHA+EPA 250–500mg/day (preferred over fish oil)
IronMODERATE — non-haem less bioavailableMonitor ferritin annually. Bisglycinate 25mg + Vit C if ferritin <40
ZincMODERATE — phytates reduce absorptionZinc bisglycinate 15–25mg/day if deficient
IodineHIGH — dairy was major sourceIodine supplement 150mcg/day (avoid variable kelp)
Vit D3HIGHLichen-derived D3 (vegan) 2000–4000 IU/day

cards.title: Renal, Cardiac & Diabetic Therapeutic Diets

rows: Emphasise plant protein — less phosphate, less acid load. Avoid high-protein supplements.
AVOID: banana, potato skin, tomato, orange, dried fruits. SAFE: apple, grapes, white rice, bread.
Avoid: dairy, cola drinks, processed foods (phosphate additives E338–341), nuts, seeds.
No added salt. Avoid processed foods, canned foods, pickles, sauces.
Fatal neurotoxicity in CKD patients — even a small amount can cause death.
<2g/day (HF: <1.5g/day). Hidden sodium: bread, processed meats, sauces, restaurant food.
Oily fish 2–3x/week (salmon, sardines, mackerel) or EPA+DHA 1–4g/day supplement
2g/day from fortified foods or supplements — LDL ↓ 10–15%

pearls.label: 🟡 Plate Method
pearls.text: Half plate = non-starchy vegetables. Quarter plate = lean protein (fish, chicken, legumes, tofu). Quarter plate = complex carbs (brown rice, quinoa, sweet potato, oats). Eat protein and fat BEFORE carbs at each meal — slows glucose absorption significantly. Post-meal 15-min walk reduces postprandial glucose 30%. Avoid: sugary drinks (single most impactful elimination), white rice and bread, processed food, fruit juices. Consistent carbohydrate distribution across meals. Intermittent fasting 16:8 (not if on insulin — hypoglycaemia risk).

cards._legacyHtml:
RENAL DIET (CKD Stages 3–5)
NutrientTargetPractical Guidance
Protein0.6–0.8g/kg/dayEmphasise plant protein — less phosphate, less acid load. Avoid high-protein supplements.
Potassium ★<2000mg/dayAVOID: banana, potato skin, tomato, orange, dried fruits. SAFE: apple, grapes, white rice, bread.
Phosphorus ★<800mg/dayAvoid: dairy, cola drinks, processed foods (phosphate additives E338–341), nuts, seeds.
Sodium<2g/dayNo added salt. Avoid processed foods, canned foods, pickles, sauces.
Star fruit ★ABSOLUTELY CONTRAINDICATEDFatal neurotoxicity in CKD patients — even a small amount can cause death.
CARDIAC DIET
TargetIntervention
Sodium<2g/day (HF: <1.5g/day). Hidden sodium: bread, processed meats, sauces, restaurant food.
Omega-3Oily fish 2–3x/week (salmon, sardines, mackerel) or EPA+DHA 1–4g/day supplement
Fibre25–35g soluble fibre (oats, legumes, psyllium) — LDL ↓ 5–10%
Plant sterols2g/day from fortified foods or supplements — LDL ↓ 10–15%
DIABETIC DIET — PLATE METHOD
🟡 Plate Method
Half plate = non-starchy vegetables. Quarter plate = lean protein (fish, chicken, legumes, tofu). Quarter plate = complex carbs (brown rice, quinoa, sweet potato, oats). Eat protein and fat BEFORE carbs at each meal — slows glucose absorption significantly. Post-meal 15-min walk reduces postprandial glucose 30%. Avoid: sugary drinks (single most impactful elimination), white rice and bread, processed food, fruit juices. Consistent carbohydrate distribution across meals. Intermittent fasting 16:8 (not if on insulin — hypoglycaemia risk).

cards.title: Ketogenic Diet — Clinical Protocol

alerts.label: Ketogenic Diet
alerts.text: Classical keto: 70-75% fat, 20-25% protein, 5% carbohydrate (under 50g net carbs/day). Induces nutritional ketosis (blood ketones 0.5-3.0 mmol/L) within 2-7 days.

rows: Grade A — 50% seizure reduction in 50% of patients
Classical 4:1 ratio under neurologist and dietitian supervision
Monitor medications closely — hypoglycaemia risk on insulin/sulphonylureas
Under 50g net carbs. Whole foods approach — not processed keto products.
6-month trial with physician monitoring. Inositol compatible adjunct.
Good — rapid reduction in hepatic fat within 2 weeks

pearls.label: 🟡 Contraindications to Keto
pearls.text: Absolute: pyruvate carboxylase deficiency, porphyria, fat oxidation disorders. Relative: kidney disease, pancreatitis history, gallbladder disease, eating disorder history. Medications needing monitoring: insulin (reduce dose), SGLT2 inhibitors (euglycaemic DKA risk — stop before starting keto), warfarin (INR shifts with dietary change).

steps.title: Keto Flu Prevention (Days 1-7)
steps.text: Sodium 3-5g/day (salt food or bouillon), potassium 1-3g/day (avocado, leafy greens), magnesium glycinate 400mg/day, hydration 3L/day. Most patients recover by day 5-7 and report significantly improved energy.

cards._legacyHtml:
Ketogenic Diet
Classical keto: 70-75% fat, 20-25% protein, 5% carbohydrate (under 50g net carbs/day). Induces nutritional ketosis (blood ketones 0.5-3.0 mmol/L) within 2-7 days.
IndicationEvidenceProtocol Notes
Drug-resistant epilepsyGrade A — 50% seizure reduction in 50% of patientsClassical 4:1 ratio under neurologist and dietitian supervision
Type 2 DiabetesStrong — significant HbA1c reductionMonitor medications closely — hypoglycaemia risk on insulin/sulphonylureas
Obesity and metabolic syndromeStrong short-term. Adherence challenging long-term.Under 50g net carbs. Whole foods approach — not processed keto products.
PCOSGood — reduces androgens, restores ovulation6-month trial with physician monitoring. Inositol compatible adjunct.
NAFLDGood — rapid reduction in hepatic fat within 2 weeks3-6 months supervised. Monitor LFTs regularly.
K
Keto Flu Prevention (Days 1-7) Sodium 3-5g/day (salt food or bouillon), potassium 1-3g/day (avocado, leafy greens), magnesium glycinate 400mg/day, hydration 3L/day. Most patients recover by day 5-7 and report significantly improved energy.
🟡 Contraindications to Keto
Absolute: pyruvate carboxylase deficiency, porphyria, fat oxidation disorders. Relative: kidney disease, pancreatitis history, gallbladder disease, eating disorder history. Medications needing monitoring: insulin (reduce dose), SGLT2 inhibitors (euglycaemic DKA risk — stop before starting keto), warfarin (INR shifts with dietary change).

cards.title: Mediterranean & Plant-Based Diets — Clinical Guide

rows: Olive oil 30ml+, vegetables 5+ servings, whole grains, legumes, fruits, nuts
Red meat under 1x/week, processed food, sugary drinks
PREDIMED trial: CVD down 30%, T2DM down 52%, Alzheimer's down 53%, breast cancer down 26%, overall mortality down 20% vs low-fat diet
Monitor ferritin annually. Iron bisglycinate 25mg if under 40 µg/L
Calcium citrate 500mg/day if dietary sources insufficient
Iodine 150mcg/day (avoid high-dose kelp — content too variable)

cards._legacyHtml:
MEDITERRANEAN DIET
ComponentDailyWeeklyRarely
Core foodsOlive oil 30ml+, vegetables 5+ servings, whole grains, legumes, fruits, nutsFish 2-3x, poultry 2x, eggs 2-4x, dairy moderateRed meat under 1x/week, processed food, sugary drinks
EvidencePREDIMED trial: CVD down 30%, T2DM down 52%, Alzheimer's down 53%, breast cancer down 26%, overall mortality down 20% vs low-fat diet
PLANT-BASED / VEGAN DIET — Essential Nutrients
NutrientRiskSupplement
Vitamin B12HIGH — only in animal foodsMethylcobalamin 1000mcg/day sublingual — ESSENTIAL, never optional
Omega-3 DHA/EPAHIGH — plant ALA converts poorlyAlgae-derived DHA+EPA 250-500mg/day
IronMODERATE — non-haem less bioavailableMonitor ferritin annually. Iron bisglycinate 25mg if under 40 µg/L
ZincMODERATE — phytates reduce absorptionZinc bisglycinate 15-25mg/day if deficient
CalciumMODERATE if no fortified foodsCalcium citrate 500mg/day if dietary sources insufficient
Vitamin DHIGH — little dietary sourceLichen-derived D3 2000-4000 IU/day
IodineHIGH — dairy was major sourceIodine 150mcg/day (avoid high-dose kelp — content too variable)

Avoid: dairy, cola drinks, processed foods (phosphate additives), nuts, seeds

pearls.label: 🟡 Plate Method
pearls.text: Half plate non-starchy vegetables. Quarter plate lean protein (fish, chicken, legumes, tofu). Quarter plate complex carbs (brown rice, quinoa, sweet potato). Consistent carbohydrate distribution across meals. Eat protein and fat BEFORE carbs — slows glucose absorption significantly. Post-meal 15-minute walk reduces postprandial glucose 30%. Avoid: sugary drinks, white rice, refined flour, processed food. Small frequent meals every 3 hours.

🟡 Plate Method
Half plate non-starchy vegetables. Quarter plate lean protein (fish, chicken, legumes, tofu). Quarter plate complex carbs (brown rice, quinoa, sweet potato). Consistent carbohydrate distribution across meals. Eat protein and fat BEFORE carbs — slows glucose absorption significantly. Post-meal 15-minute walk reduces postprandial glucose 30%. Avoid: sugary drinks, white rice, refined flour, processed food. Small frequent meals every 3 hours.
